content: Budgets fields max_cost_usd and max_tool_retries are declared in packages/vigor-core/src/vigor_core/schemas.py:57-58 but not read by the orchestrator (verified via grep over packages/). Only max_iterations, max_candidates, and max_wall_clock_s are enforced (orchestrator.py:114, :263, :116).

Orchestrator.run uses asyncio.gather exactly once: orchestrator.py:507 fans out adapter reviewers vs backend review for a single candidate. Best-of-N generation (orchestrator.py:262-275) and per-candidate evaluation (orchestrator.py:122-128) are sequential 'for ... await' loops — Phase 4 'parallel candidate scheduling' (docs/roadmap.md:70) is the natural seam to parallelize the evaluation loop first (no inter-candidate data dependency) before generation (which depends on the running prior_candidates list).

Orchestrator.run has a single iteration-boundary enforcement seam at packages/vigor-runtime/src/vigor_runtime/orchestrator.py:114-118 where ALL new resource budgets (ADR-0028 cost, ADR-0031 parallel, ADR-0033 checkpoint) plug in identically. The pattern is: per-iteration check before _candidate_batch, break with stop_reason on threshold, no in-flight cancellation. This is the architectural shape; future budget axes (max_input_tokens, max_tool_calls) plug into the same seam.

name: iteration-boundary-budget-tracker-pattern
description: Iteration-boundary budget enforcement: orchestrator.py:121-123 adds a sibling check next to the max_wall_clock_s seam (orchestrator.py:118). Pattern is generalizable to any future budget axis (max_tool_calls, max_input_tokens) — instantiate a tracker before the loop, poll await tracker.check() at the iteration boundary, set stop_reason=<axis>_exceeded on overrun. Bounded overshoot of one iteration is the documented trade-off (ADR-0028 §Negative #3). Tracker objects also poll once in finally to populate the final telemetry on RunResult, even when no enforcement was configured.

name: parallel-fanout-id-restamp
description: Parallel asyncio.gather fanout over LLM backend calls requires post-gather candidate-id re-stamp. Backends derive cand_<task>_<NNNN> from len(prior_candidates), but every coroutine in a parallel chunk sees the same prior_candidates snapshot, so a 4-call chunk collides on slot 0000. Orchestrator pre-computes the global slot index per call and overwrites result.ir.candidate_id via model_copy after gather returns. Backends stay simple (no API change); determinism in IDs is preserved even though on-disk write order becomes nondeterministic (ADR-0034 §Negative §2). Pattern is reusable for any future fanout site that calls a stateless backend within an iteration.

name: observer-protocol-seam
description: ADR-0037 RuntimeObserver Protocol: @runtime_checkable Protocol type lives in vigor_core.observability (not vigor_runtime — keeps downstream observer packages from depending on the runtime). Orchestrator __init__ does ONE isinstance check at construction (fail-fast on partial observers); per-emission hot path is fast-path 'if observer is None: return' before any attribute lookup. Observer methods are sync (async would let a slow observer stall the event loop); concurrent emission under ADR-0034 parallel best-of-N is documented as observer responsibility. Try/except wraps every emission via private _emit helper so observer bugs cannot break runs — exceptions logged at WARNING under 'vigor.runtime' logger.

content: Orthogonal observability surfaces in Orchestrator.run: every lifecycle emission point (on_run_start/on_iteration_start/on_candidate_start/on_candidate_end/on_iteration_end/on_run_end/on_event) is paired with a logging.getLogger('vigor.runtime') call at INFO (run+iteration boundaries) or DEBUG (candidate boundaries). Two independent surfaces — one structured (observer Protocol, Pydantic objects), one human (formatted log strings) — so library users can pick either or both without coupling them. Pattern set by ADR-0037 §Decision Outcome paragraph 'Logging is orthogonal'.
